epigraph.text: The average man does not want to be free. He simply wants to be safe.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Senator Votes for Bill Requiring His Own Resignation
follyOfTheDay.summary: In a stunning display of legislative incompetence, a U.S. senator cast an affirmative vote on a bill he believed addressed rural infrastructure funding. The measure's actual title: "An Act to Remove Officeholders Who Demonstrate Incompetence Through Absence of Basic Reading Comprehension." The senator proceeded to read the full title aloud on the Senate floor approximately thirty seconds after his vote was recorded. He discovered this error after the bill had already passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, leading colleagues to note that seldom has an elected official so thoroughly endorsed legislation explicitly designed to remove himself from office.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: Few things demonstrate the self-aware irony of democratic institutions quite like a legislator voting to abolish his own position due to an inability to read.

treadwellCorner.headline: Man Attempts to Teach Raccoon Culinary Skills, Loses Kitchen to Fire
treadwellCorner.summary: After watching cooking shows, a Louisville resident attempted to train a wild raccoon to prepare meals. The raccoon's confusion with a gas stove resulted in multiple burners igniting simultaneously, destroying the kitchen and causing $180,000 in damages.

campusWatch.headline: University Bans Pencils as 'Inherently Violent Objects'
campusWatch.summary: A northeastern college's Office of Inclusive Excellence ruled that pencils, due to their pointed tips, constitute weapons of potential harm. Students must now submit assignments in crayon or soft marker only.

campusWatch.headline: College Mandates Apology Sessions for Students Who Sneeze
campusWatch.summary: A Massachusetts liberal arts school now requires 45-minute workshops for any student who sneezes during lectures, arguing that sneezing violates the psychological safety of neurodivergent peers.
